summary: Map all touch points + plan: rank map+list by high-precision percentileRank, decouple selection from rank, fix tap-highlight color

findings.area: API high-precision Crave-score plumbing (percentileRank) for map/list rank agreement
findings.currentBehavior:
The LIVE search flow is the dual-query path only: search.service.ts:1717 calls queryExecutor.executeDual. The single-query execute() (search-query.executor.ts:295) and its mappers mapFoodResults (executor:1006) / mapRestaurantResults (executor:1076) are DEAD — grep for `.execute(` / `queryExecutor.execute` finds no live caller; only executeDual is used. So changes only need to touch the dual path, but the dead path can be updated for consistency.

Every live result row's craveScore comes from the ROUNDED DB column display_score (Decimal(5,1)), and every ORDER BY is on display_score, so two ~99.9 restaurants tie and the map vs list break the tie differently. The high-precision percentile_rank (Decimal(6,5), non-nullable) already exists on core_public_entity_scores (schema.prisma:182) but is never SELECTed nor sent to mobile.

SQL is in search-query.builder.ts. The two CTEs public_restaurant_scores (builder:1393-1414) and public_connection_scores (builder:1427-1448) SELECT only subject_id, display_score, score_delta_7d — they DROP percentile_rank, so the exact value is gone before any downstream alias. Restaurant LIST query: buildRestaurantQuery (builder:108) → ranked_restaurants CTE (builder:251-297) selects prs.display_score AS crave_score (builder:261); ORDER BY = resolveRestaurantOrderSql → prs.display_score DESC, COALESCE(rvt.total_upvotes,0) DESC, fr.entity_id ASC (builder:1590-1595). Map PIN query: buildDishQuery (builder:463) → filtered_connections CTE (builder:558-606) selects pcs.display_score AS connection_crave_score (builder:569) and prs.display_score AS restaurant_crave_score (builder:581); ORDER BY = resolveDishOrderSql → fc.connection_crave_score DESC, fc.total_upvotes DESC, fc.mention_count DESC, fc.connection_id ASC (builder:1567-1572).

Row→DTO (executor): mapRestaurantQueryResults (executor:2317; craveScore parsed at 2395-2398 via toRequiredPublicScore; server rank assigned at 2392 = rankStart+index) builds RestaurantResultDto for the LIST. mapDishQueryResults (executor:2435; connection craveScore 2471-2474, restaurant craveScore 2488-2491) builds FoodResultDto for MAP PINS. Both row types (DishQueryRow executor:166-202, RestaurantQueryRow executor:121-161) declare the score fields and must add the new column.

The response DTOs extend shared interfaces (apps/api/.../dto/search-query.dto.ts:271 FoodResultDto extends SharedFoodResult, :278 RestaurantResultDto extends SharedRestaurantResult, :276 RestaurantFoodSnippetDto = SharedRestaurantFoodSnippet). The actual wire shape is defined in packages/shared/src/types/search.ts: FoodResult.craveScore (search.ts:47), RestaurantResult.craveScore (search.ts:117), RestaurantFoodSnippet.craveScore (search.ts:72). These shared types ARE the serialization contract sent to mobile (NestJS returns plain objects, no class-transformer @Expose gate found on these — they pass through). craveScore is a plain number, so adding a sibling number field flows automatically.

Parse helpers already handle Decimal: executor toRequiredPublicScore/toOptionalNumber and coverage requirePublicScore/optionalNumber both .toNumber() on Decimal — percentileRank Decimal(6,5) parses fine.

The COVERAGE map endpoint (the dots layer) is a separate path: search-coverage.service.ts builds GeoJSON properties directly (one builder, coverage:280-336): selects prs.display_score AS crave_score (coverage:259), top dish via buildTopDishJoinSql pcs.display_score AS crave_score (coverage:369), ORDER BY display_score (coverage:178-179), and stamps properties.craveScore / properties.rank = index+1 (coverage:317/323). It uses inline CTEs public_restaurant_scores (coverage:244-248) / public_connection_scores (coverage:249-253) that also only select display_score.

The FAVORITES path also builds the same RestaurantResult/FoodResult shapes and renders on the map: favorite-lists.service.ts mapRestaurantResults (658), mapFoodResults (766), loadPublicScores uses Prisma .findMany select {displayScore, scoreDelta7d} (favorites:625-636), type FavoritePublicScore = Pick<...,'subjectId'|'displayScore'|'scoreDelta7d'> (favorites:52-55), toPublicScoreValue returns Number(score.displayScore) (favorites:639-650).

MOBILE consumers (out of scope for THIS API task, but the new field is what they will re-rank by): feature.properties.craveScore is built from dish.craveScore / restaurant.craveScore in apps/mobile/.../runtime/map/map-read-model-builder.ts (lines 81/120, properties at 94/146); the map re-ranks in apps/mobile/.../hooks/use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts (rank read at ~1546, badge at ~1778-1783); the list uses row.rank.

packages/shared/src/types/search.ts:117 — add craveScoreExact?: number to RestaurantResult (results list)
packages/shared/src/types/search.ts:72 — OPTIONAL: add craveScoreExact?: number to RestaurantFoodSnippet (topFood; only if topFood ever needs independent ranking — not required for the map/list goal)

DEAD-PATH (optional, for consistency, NOT live): search-query.builder.ts ranked_restaurants/QueryResultRow single-query shape; executor mapFoodResults (1006) / mapRestaurantResults (1076); search.service.ts getRestaurantDishes (1186-1208) / getRestaurantProfile (1519) / getPublicRestaurantScore (1632) — these are detail/profile, not the ranked search; leave unless you want craveScoreExact everywhere
proposedChanges: Add the high-precision column at the lowest common point: in buildPublicRestaurantScoresCte and buildPublicConnectionScoresCte (builder:1393-1448) add `percentile_rank` to each SELECT (sql + preview). This is the cleanest single source — both buildRestaurantQuery and buildDishQuery join these CTEs, so the value becomes available to all downstream CTEs.

In ranked_restaurants CTE (builder:261/304) alias `prs.percentile_rank AS crave_score_exact`. In filtered_connections CTE (builder:569/611 and 581/617) alias `pcs.percentile_rank AS connection_crave_score_exact` and `prs.percentile_rank AS restaurant_crave_score_exact`.

DO NOT change any ORDER BY. The API keeps returning rows ordered by display_score (resolveRestaurantOrderSql/resolveDishOrderSql unchanged) and keeps the server-assigned `rank` (executor:2392). Mobile re-orders by the new exact field client-side per the product goal. (Alternative if you'd rather the API order match too: change ORDER BY to percentile_rank DESC, display_score DESC, <stable id> — but the task says keep user-visible API order, so prefer leaving it.)
Add `crave_score_exact` / `connection_crave_score_exact` to the row interfaces RestaurantQueryRow (executor:121-161) and DishQueryRow (executor:166-202).

In mapRestaurantQueryResults (executor:2395) and mapDishQueryResults (executor:2471) populate a new DTO field `craveScoreExact: this.toRequiredPublicScore(row.<exact>, label)` using the existing Decimal-aware parser (no rounding).

Add `craveScoreExact?: number` to the SHARED types FoodResult (search.ts:47) and RestaurantResult (search.ts:117); rebuild packages/shared so apps/api and apps/mobile both see the field. Make it optional (`?`) so the deprecated/detail paths that don't set it still typecheck. These shared interfaces are the serialization contract — NestJS returns the plain objects, so the field ships automatically once set.

Coverage map (dots): in search-coverage.service.ts add percentile_rank to inline CTEs (244-253), to the main SELECT (259) as crave_score_exact, and to buildTopDishJoinSql (369) as crave_score_exact; extend CoverageRestaurantRow (12-23); stamp `craveScoreExact` (and `topDishCraveScoreExact`) into GeoJSON properties (314-333). NOTE coverage uses ad-hoc property objects, not the shared GeoJSON type, so no shared-type change is strictly needed there — but mirror the FoodResult naming.

Favorites: extend FavoritePublicScore Pick to include 'percentileRank' (favorites:52-55), add `percentileRank: true` to the two Prisma selects in loadPublicScores (625-636), and set `craveScoreExact: Number(score.percentileRank)` in mapRestaurantResults (725) and mapFoodResults (790).

Recommended naming: use percentileRank verbatim as the wire field name OR `craveScoreExact` consistently across shared types + all set sites. Decimal(6,5) is 0..1; if mobile compares numerically for ordering, the raw fraction is fine (higher = better, matches display_score direction). Send it as a number (Number(decimal)) — do not round.
findings.risks: "Low product risk: adding an optional field is additive and backward-compatible (older mobile clients ignore it; the existing craveScore/rank/order are unchanged). Because the task says keep the user-visible API order, the LIST and MAP rows still arrive ordered by display_score and the server `rank` is unchanged — so until mobile is updated to re-sort by craveScoreExact, behavior is identical (the bug persists, by design, on old clients). Things that could break: (1) percentile_rank is NON-nullable in schema so toRequiredPublicScore won't throw for live scored rows, but any row joined to a score row that somehow lacks it would now throw — same failure mode as today's craveScore, so no new surface. (2) Must add the column in BOTH the Prisma `sql` AND the `preview` string of every CTE/SELECT in the builder, or the SQL-preview debug output diverges (preview is only used for logging, not execution, so a miss is cosmetic). (3) Two restaurants can still tie if they share an identical percentile_rank AND display_score — keep a deterministic final tiebreak (stable id) in whatever sort mobile applies so map and list still agree. (4) Decimal(6,5) precision: percentile_rank distinguishes far more finely than display_score, so it WILL re-order some near-ties relative to today's display_score order — that is the intended fix, but it means the map badge numbers and list positions may shift for ~tied restaurants vs the current build; expected and desired. (5) Remember to rebuild packages/shared (dist) — apps consume the built types; editing only src without rebuild can desync TS. (6) Selection must NOT change badge rank: that is a mobile concern (VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY selected=400 in use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts) — no API change needed; native force-promote via highlightedMarkerKeys already handles promotion. The API change here is purely to SUPPLY the high-precision number."

findings.area: Mobile MAP rank pipeline + selection decouple (apps/mobile/src/screens/Search). Goal: rank BOTH map pins and results list by a high-precision Crave score (percentileRank DESC, tie-break display_score DESC, then stable restaurantId) so the map badge number == the list position; and make selection NEVER reorder the badge rank (only native force-promote + recolor, both already implemented).
findings.currentBehavior:
RANK ORIGIN (single source for BOTH surfaces): every map feature's `rank`/`craveScore` is stamped from `restaurant.rank`/`restaurant.craveScore` in buildMarkerCatalogReadModel (map-read-model-builder.ts:115 reads canonicalRestaurantRankById.get(restaurant.restaurantId); :120 craveScore = restaurant.craveScore; :144-149 writes properties). `restaurant.rank` is the API-assigned ordinal (ORDER BY display_score) and `restaurant.craveScore` is display_score (Decimal(5,1), rounded). The list reads the SAME `row.rank` directly (search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1097). So both already share one rank — the problem is that one shared rank is the DISPLAY-score ordering, which ties at ~99.9 and the API tie-break differs from the map's.

MAP RE-RANK + THE SELECTION BUG: per publish, collectSearchMapVisualCandidates (use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:332) merges sources, dedups by visual identity, then SORTS (lines 375-386) by: (1) VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY DESC, (2) feature.properties.rank ASC, (3) order/markerKey. The sorted POSITION is then re-assigned as rank=index+1 at two sites: the catalog/native-promotion rank (line 1519) and the badge/reveal-seed rank (line 1687, fed into unifiedRankByKey at 1694 and the badge at 1787-1794). VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY (lines 288-294) gives selected=400; resolveEffectiveVisualSourceKind (310-313) flips the tapped restaurant's sourceKind to 'selected' (and restaurantOnlyId to 'restaurant_only'=500), so on tap it sorts to position 0 → its re-indexed rank becomes 1 (THE BUG: tapped pin's badge jumps to #1). Because the sort's primary key is sourcePriority and only its SECONDARY key is rank, even without selection the order is NOT a pure Crave-score order — it is rank-within-priority-bucket; and rank itself is display_score-derived, so two 99.9 ties resolve by markerKey, diverging from the API/list tie-break.

NATIVE PROMOTION + SELECTION RECOLOR ARE ALREADY DECOUPLED: native reads the JS-published catalog `rank` (publishCandidateCatalog, line 1557) and promotes the top-N by it (SearchMapRenderController.swift:1423/1437-1441, with the explicit comment at :1434 that once JS sorts the catalog by Crave score, that rank == the badge). The tapped pin is force-promoted natively via highlightedMarkerKeys (built in search-map.tsx:2049-2092 off effectiveSelectedRestaurantId, independent of the JS sort) → JS does NOT need sourcePriority to promote it. Pin/dot color is baked into the sprite by score bucket (rankBadgeImageId → scoreToBucket, quality-color.ts:118-125; dotBucketImageId :141), and the selected/highlight recolor rides highlightedMarkerKeys natively — also independent of the JS rank order. craveScore precision: feature.properties.craveScore is typed `number` (search-map.tsx:782) and currently carries display_score only; there is NO high-precision field on RestaurantResult (packages/shared/src/types/search.ts:106-142) or RestaurantFeatureProperties (search-map.tsx:779-815).

apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/shared/use-search-results-panel-restaurant-card-metrics-runtime.ts:27,83 + use-search-results-panel-card-render-runtime.tsx:83,149-151 (LIST rank from restaurant.rank — must equal map rank: keep both sourced from one server rank OR re-rank list by same craveScoreExact)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/shared/search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1097 (list card descriptor rank = row.rank)

apps/api/src/modules/search/search.service.ts:1186,1208 + search-coverage.service.ts:259,268,323 + search-query.executor.ts (API must SELECT pcs.percentile_rank and ORDER BY it, and emit the new field — out of this AREA's scope but the prerequisite for the exact score to exist on mobile)
proposedChanges: Add a high-precision score field to the feature properties. In RestaurantFeatureProperties (search-map.tsx:779-815) add `craveScoreExact?: number | null` (carry percentileRank, the Decimal(6,5) high-precision field). In map-read-model-builder.ts:144-150 (restaurant branch) and :91-101 (dish branch) stamp `craveScoreExact: restaurant.craveScoreExact ?? null`. Source it from a new RestaurantResult field (packages/shared/src/types/search.ts) populated by the API (percentile_rank); for coverage-sourced features also carry it through the native-query re-projection at use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:2701-2725.

Change the RANK SORT, not the dedup. In collectSearchMapVisualCandidates (use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:375-386) replace the comparator's primary key: sort by craveScoreExact DESC (higher first), then tie-break display_score (feature.properties.craveScore) DESC, then a STABLE id (restaurantId, then markerKey). REMOVE VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY and feature.properties.rank from THIS sort entirely. Concretely the new comparator: `(b.exact - a.exact) || (b.display - a.display) || a.restaurantId.localeCompare(b.restaurantId) || a.markerKey.localeCompare(b.markerKey)`, reading exact via a null-safe coalesce (missing exact sorts last). This makes the sorted position == pure Crave-score rank.

KEEP VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY only in shouldReplaceVisualCandidate (lines 316-330) where it decides WHICH duplicate feature (selected vs viewport vs coverage) wins the single visual-identity slot — that is dedup identity, not ORDER, and must stay so the selected restaurant's preferred feature/coordinate is the one kept. resolveEffectiveVisualSourceKind (296-314) stays as-is; once the sort ignores priority, flipping a feature to 'selected'/'restaurant_only' no longer moves its rank — only its dedup precedence.

Both re-index sites (1519, 1687) need NO logic change — they already do rank=index+1 over the now-Crave-score-ordered array, so the catalog rank (→ native promotion, line 1557), unifiedRankByKey (1694), promotedSeedKeys (1736), and the badge rank (1787-1794) all become the pure Crave-score rank automatically and stay mutually consistent (the existing one-rank invariant is preserved). Native promotion (Swift:1437) and the badge agree by construction — matches the in-code note at Swift:1434.

Make the RESULTS LIST agree. Two options: (A) preferred — have the API ORDER BY percentile_rank DESC, tie-break display_score DESC, then a stable id, and assign `rank` from that order, so restaurant.rank (consumed by the list at search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1097 and the metrics runtimes) already equals the map's Crave-score order with zero list-side change; OR (B) if the API rank can't change, re-rank the list client-side by the SAME craveScoreExact DESC / display DESC / stable-id comparator used by the map (in use-search-results-panel-restaurant-card-metrics-runtime.ts:27 / compute-marker-pipeline.ts:49 where canonicalRestaurantRankById is built) so both surfaces use one comparator. (A) is cleaner and keeps map+list trivially in sync.

Selection decouple is already satisfied on the native side (force-promote + recolor via highlightedMarkerKeys, search-map.tsx:2049). After removing sourcePriority from the rank sort, no JS change is needed to stop selection from reordering the badge — verify via the [RANKDIAG-v2] probe (lines 1526-1533) that tapping a pin no longer makes its mapRank #1 and that mapRank#1 == list#1 (e.g. Mia's, not Caffè Panna).
findings.risks: "BACKWARD COMPAT / PREREQUISITE: the exact score does not exist on mobile today — RestaurantResult and the API payload carry only display_score. Until the API SELECTs/emits percentile_rank, craveScoreExact is null and the new comparator falls back to display_score DESC + stable id; that ALONE fixes the map-vs-list tie divergence (deterministic stable tie-break on both surfaces) even before high precision lands, but does NOT achieve true precision ordering — both pieces are needed for the stated goal. Coordinate the shared-type field add with the API change so old/new clients don't disagree. PRODUCT-VISIBLE: badge numbers will shift for any restaurant whose display-score rank differed from its percentile rank (intended), and the tapped pin will no longer show #1 (intended fix) — confirm with stakeholders this is the desired UX. CORRECTNESS: removing rank from the candidate SORT is safe because the position is immediately re-assigned as the rank (1519/1687) and native reads that published position rank (Swift:1557→1437), so the two-source rank-collision bug the old sort guarded against is still handled by the dedup + position re-rank; but the sort comparator MUST be fully deterministic (always end on a stable id) or pins/list can disagree frame-to-frame. Keep VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY in shouldReplaceVisualCandidate — dropping it there would change which duplicate feature/coordinate is kept for the selected restaurant (multi-location pin pick) and could move the selected pin's location. Verify the precomputed catalog path (orderByEntry, map-read-model-builder.ts:24 + compute-marker-pipeline) also orders by the same key, else the precomputed-vs-fallback catalog could rank differently. Re-validate with the LOD harness (badge==promotion rank, no wiggle) since the badge sprite ids change."

findings.area: Mobile RESULTS LIST ordering + rank badge (apps/mobile/src/screens/Search)
findings.currentBehavior:
The results list follows the API order verbatim and reads the API's server-assigned rank for the badge — it does NOT sort by anything itself.

Order chain (list never re-sorts; only slices into Exact/Broader sections, preserving order):
- Rows originate from the raw API array `mountedResults.restaurants` at apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/shared/search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:512-514 (passed to buildSearchResultsSectionedProjection as `restaurants: mountedResults.restaurants ?? []`).
- apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/read-models/results-read-model-sectioned-projection.ts:43-65 -> buildSafeResultsData only filters null/undefined and preserves order (apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/read-models/list-read-model-builder.ts:43-60); buildSectionedResultsData only slice()s the array into "Exact matches"/"Broader matches" sections, never sorts (list-read-model-builder.ts:88-122). So the displayed order == the order the API put restaurants in (today: ORDER BY pcs.display_score DESC).

Rank badge value (independent of array position; comes straight from the API field):
- apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/shared/search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1097 reads `row.rank` (`const rank = typeof row.rank === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.rank) ? row.rank : null`) and passes it as the card descriptor's `rank` (line 1108). If `row.rank` is null the row is dropped (lines 1098-1100). That `rank` is RestaurantResult.rank, declared server-assigned/1-based at packages/shared/src/types/search.ts:114.

Key cross-check with the map: the MAP reads the SAME RestaurantResult.rank field for its canonical rank — apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/map/compute-marker-pipeline.ts:50-57 builds canonicalRestaurantRankById from `restaurant.rank`, fed into the feature as feature.properties.rank (apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/map/map-read-model-builder.ts:115,148). So list rank and map rank share one source. The map/list #1 divergence is therefore NOT a list-ordering difference; it is (a) the API tie at display_score 99.9 yielding an ambiguous server rank, and (b) the map re-indexing the SELECTED pin to rank 1 via sourceKind='selected' priority 400 (use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:316-330, 375-386, re-index at 1519-1522) which the list never does. craveScore on RestaurantResult (packages/shared/src/types/search.ts:117) is the rounded display_score; there is no high-precision field on the type today.
touchPoints: packages/shared/src/types/search.ts:117 — add a high-precision field (e.g. craveScoreExact: number) to RestaurantResult so the API can ship percentileRank/raw to the client (consumed by both list and map)

apps/api/src/modules/search/search.service.ts (~1186 SELECT, ~1208 ORDER BY, toRequiredPublicScore ~1167/1286/1620) and apps/api/src/modules/search/search-query.executor.ts (~1032/1131/2395/2471/2523) and apps/api/src/modules/search/search-coverage.service.ts — must (a) select pcs.percentile_rank, (b) ORDER BY pcs.percentile_rank DESC, pcs.display_score DESC, then a stable id, and (c) assign rank by that high-precision order; this is the upstream that decides BOTH list order and the rank field the list/map read

apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/shared/search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1097 — `row.rank` read for the badge (will be the high-precision-derived server rank once API changes; no change needed here if the API assigns rank by the new order)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/shared/search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:512-514 — list order source (raw API restaurants array; relies on API ordering it by the high-precision key)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/read-models/results-read-model-sectioned-projection.ts:43-65 — projection that preserves API order (verify still order-preserving)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/read-models/list-read-model-builder.ts:43-122 — buildSafeResultsData/buildSectionedResultsData (order-preserving; no sort to add IF the API orders)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/map/compute-marker-pipeline.ts:50-57 — map canonicalRestaurantRankById from restaurant.rank (shares the same rank source as the list)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/runtime/map/map-read-model-builder.ts:115,146-148 — feature.properties.rank/craveScore (would carry craveScoreExact for tie-break/color parity)

apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/hooks/use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:316-330,375-386,1519-1522 — selection priority bump + re-index that currently makes the selected pin badge become 1 (must be removed so selection only force-promotes natively, not re-ranks)
proposedChanges: Make the SERVER the single source of order+rank: order results by percentile_rank DESC, tie-break display_score DESC, then a stable id, and assign each restaurant.rank by that order. The list ALREADY honors restaurant.rank for its badge and ALREADY renders in API array order, so if the API ships restaurants in this order with rank assigned to match, the list badge == list position == map rank automatically — no list-side ordering code change required.

Do NOT add a client-side sort to the list. The cleanest design keeps the list a pure consumer of API order + restaurant.rank (search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:512-514 and :1097 stay as-is). Adding a JS sort would duplicate ranking logic and risk drifting from the map; instead guarantee the API order is the high-precision order.

If a defensive client guarantee is wanted (so list order can never disagree with restaurant.rank even if the API array order regresses), add a single stable sort in resolveMountedRowsProjection/buildSafeResultsData keyed by restaurant.rank ascending (list-read-model-builder.ts:43-60). This makes the displayed position == restaurant.rank by construction. Keep it minimal and only for restaurants (dishes unaffected).

To finish parity with the map's high-precision tie-break and color, thread the new high-precision field (craveScoreExact) through RestaurantResult -> feature.properties so the map can tie-break on it and the list could too if ever needed; but for list ORDERING the rank field alone is sufficient once the API assigns it by percentile_rank.

Remove the map's selection-time re-rank so badge never changes on tap: drop the 'selected' sourceKind priority influence on ordering (use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts shouldReplaceVisualCandidate:316-330 and the sort 375-386) and the index+1 re-assignment for the selected case (1519-1522) — selection should only force-promote natively (highlightedMarkerKeys) and recolor, leaving feature.properties.rank = the high-precision server rank, matching the list.
findings.risks: "Backward-compat: adding craveScoreExact to RestaurantResult is additive (optional), low risk; older clients ignore it. Changing the API ORDER BY changes the result order for ALL clients — if any other consumer assumes display_score ordering or relies on the old tie-break, their order shifts (web, favorites, coverage paths share toRequiredPublicScore). Re-assigning rank by percentile_rank means restaurants previously rank-1 by display_score tie may move; product-visible badge numbers shift for tied/near-tied restaurants (this is the intended fix). If the API does NOT reliably emit restaurants in rank order, the list (which trusts array order) and the badge (row.rank) could disagree — mitigated by the optional defensive sort-by-rank in list-read-model-builder.ts. Rows with missing/non-finite rank are silently dropped at search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1098-1100, so the API MUST always assign rank or those restaurants vanish from the list. Removing the map selection re-rank could regress any code that assumed the selected pin is feature.properties.rank 1 (e.g. native top-N promotion at 1519); selection promotion must stay via highlightedMarkerKeys/forcedKeys (already native) so the demoted-but-selected pin still shows. Floating-point ties on percentile_rank Decimal(6,5) are far less likely than display_score Decimal(5,1), but the stable-id final tie-break must be deterministic and identical on map and list to guarantee agreement."

findings.area: Map tap-highlight COLOR bug: a force-promoted (selected) pin keeps its normal bucket/rank color instead of recoloring to the active/highlight color under LOD v5. The pin's name-label DOES turn active-color and the dot WOULD turn highlighted, but the visible pin sprite never changes.
findings.currentBehavior:
ROOT CAUSE (proven by code, not theory): the `nativeHighlighted` feature-state IS written correctly to the pin's physical source, but the PIN LAYER's iconImage expression NEVER reads it, and there is no highlighted pin sprite to switch to. The dot and the label both read `nativeHighlighted`; only the pin omits it. So this is NOT "nativeHighlighted never written" and NOT "v5 bake-0/feature-state path bypasses it" — it is purely a missing expression branch (and missing sprite) on the pin layer.

Evidence:
- DOT recolors on highlight: search-map.tsx:2390 `iconImage: ['case', nativeHighlightedExpression, 'dot-highlighted', ['get','dotImageId']]`, where nativeHighlightedExpression = `['==', ['coalesce', ['feature-state','nativeHighlighted'], 0], 1]` (search-map.tsx:2378-2381). The `dot-highlighted` sprite exists (apps/mobile/src/assets/dots/dot-highlighted.png, registered via DOT_IMAGE_ENTRIES at search-map.tsx:675).
- LABEL recolors on highlight: use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:2933 `textColor: ['case', nativeHighlightedExpression, ACTIVE_TAB_COLOR_DARK, '#374151']` (its own copy of the expression at :2907-2911).
- PIN does NOT: stylePinSingleSymbolStyle.iconImage at search-map.tsx:2623-2645 is `['coalesce', ['get','badgeImageId'], <score-bucket step fallback>]`. No `['case', nativeHighlighted, ...]` branch and no iconColor. It always paints the normal-color badge sprite (`pin-rank-b<bucket>-<rank>`).
- The feature-state IS written (rules out "never written"): applyHighlightedMarkerState (SearchMapRenderController.swift:9519-9573) iterates visualSourceIds — which returns the PHYSICAL sources [pinBundleSourceId, labelRenderSourceId, dotSourceId, labelCollisionSourceId] (:9907-9908) — and calls setFeatureState with `"nativeHighlighted": isHighlighted ? 1 : 0` (:9563-9569). It's invoked from setHighlightedMarker (:2959) AND from the per-frame snapshot apply (:2493). Pins render from pinBundleSourceId, and the v5 opacity writer also targets `pinPhysicalSourceId = state.pinBundleSourceId` (:8501, :8553), so the feature-state lands on exactly the source the pin layer reads from. The label render-source (labelRenderSourceId) is in the same list, which is why the label color already flips correctly.
- No highlighted pin sprite exists: the generated badge set has ONLY `pin-rank-*` and `pin-score-*` ids (apps/mobile/src/generated/pin-badge-images.ts) — grep for highlight/active/selected returns nothing. The pin badge PNGs are full-color and registered WITHOUT `sdf: true` (PIN_BADGE_IMAGE_ENTRIES at search-map.tsx:673; contrast STYLE_PIN_FILL_IMAGE_ID at :669 which IS `sdf: true`), so a Mapbox `iconColor` tint cannot recolor them.

Why the v5 angle in the question is a red herring: v5 changed WHO writes nativeLodOpacity (the LodEngine via feature-state, see applyV5OpacityWrites :8489-8569), not the highlight path. applyV5OpacityWrites writes nativeLodOpacity/nativeDotOpacity/label-opacity but never touches nativeHighlighted, and applyHighlightedMarkerState is a separate, still-live writer. v5's bake-0 only zeros the opacity baked-fallback, which is orthogonal to the highlight icon swap. The pin has been colorblind to nativeHighlighted since the single-symbol baked-badge design landed — v5 just didn't add the missing branch.
touchPoints: apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/components/search-map.tsx:2623-2645 (stylePinSingleSymbolStyle.iconImage — MUST add the nativeHighlighted branch)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/components/search-map.tsx:2660 (useMemo deps for stylePinSingleSymbolStyle — add nativeHighlightedExpression)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/components/search-map.tsx:2378-2381 (nativeHighlightedExpression — reuse this exact coalesce so pin matches dot/label)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/components/search-map.tsx:665-676 (MapboxGL.Images registration — register the new highlighted pin sprite(s))
apps/mobile/src/generated/pin-badge-images.ts (generated badge id list — must include highlighted pin sprite ids if going the sprite route)
scripts/generate-pin-bucket-sprites.js (sprite generator — bake the active-color pin variant; canonical colors at :58-65)
apps/mobile/src/utils/quality-color.ts:118-125 (rankBadgeImageId — or a sibling helper to resolve the highlighted badge id)

apps/mobile/ios/cravesearch/SearchMapRenderController.swift:9519-9573 (applyHighlightedMarkerState — already correct, writes nativeHighlighted to pinBundleSourceId; no change needed, cited to prove the state IS written)
apps/mobile/ios/cravesearch/SearchMapRenderController.swift:9907-9908 (visualSourceIds — confirms physical pin source is covered)
apps/mobile/ios/cravesearch/SearchMapRenderController.swift:2493 and :2959 (call sites — confirm highlight apply still runs under v5)
apps/mobile/src/screens/Search/hooks/use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:2933 (label textColor — already correct reference pattern)
proposedChanges: PRIMARY FIX (pin icon swap, mirrors the dot exactly). In search-map.tsx wrap the existing badge resolution in a highlight case. Replace stylePinSingleSymbolStyle.iconImage (search-map.tsx:2623-2645) with: `['case', nativeHighlightedExpression, <highlighted-pin-image>, ['coalesce', ['get','badgeImageId'], <existing score-bucket step fallback>]]`. Add `nativeHighlightedExpression` to that useMemo's deps (search-map.tsx:2660). Reuse the SAME nativeHighlightedExpression already defined at search-map.tsx:2378-2381 so the pin flips on the identical feature-state the dot and label use.

SPRITE STRATEGY — the highlighted pin must still show the SAME baked rank number (the badge rank must not change on selection, per the product goal), so a single flat 'pin-highlighted' sprite (like dot-highlighted) is NOT enough; it would erase the number. Bake an active-color variant PER RANK/bucket: add an active palette pass to scripts/generate-pin-bucket-sprites.js (canonical colors at :58-65 + an ACTIVE color), emitting e.g. `pin-rank-active-<rank>` (number preserved, body painted active color). Add a resolver in quality-color.ts (sibling to rankBadgeImageId at :118-125), e.g. `activeRankBadgeImageId(rank)`, and use it as the highlighted branch image. Register the new sprites in MapboxGL.Images (search-map.tsx:665-676) and add their ids to apps/mobile/src/generated/pin-badge-images.ts.

CHEAPER ALTERNATIVE if a per-rank active bake is too heavy: add a single transparent active-color RING/HALO overlay sprite drawn in a SECOND pin layer (like the existing shadow layer at search-map.tsx:1233/STYLE_PIN_SHADOW_IMAGE_ID) gated on `nativeHighlightedExpression` via iconOpacity, leaving the numbered badge sprite untouched. This recolors the pin's perceived state without re-baking 1683 sprites. Pick this if 'recolor' can mean an active ring/outline rather than a full body repaint.

DO NOT use iconColor to tint the badge: the badge PNGs are non-SDF full-color (search-map.tsx:673, no sdf flag), so iconColor is a no-op on them. Only the SDF pin-fill (search-map.tsx:669) is tintable, but that layer is not the rendered pin under the single-symbol design.

NO native change required: applyHighlightedMarkerState already writes nativeHighlighted to pinBundleSourceId for every feature (SearchMapRenderController.swift:9519-9573), it runs on selection (:2959) and every snapshot frame (:2493), and the pin reads feature-state from that exact physical source. Verify post-fix with the LOD harness: tap a demoted marker, confirm the pin's painted icon switches to the active sprite while roleP/renderP and badge rank are unchanged (selection must NOT change rank — see the parallel ranking work).
findings.risks: "Low blast radius — the change is additive (a new case branch + new sprites); the non-highlighted branch is byte-identical to today's expression, so unselected pins are unaffected. Risks: (1) Sprite-bake route adds 1683+ more PNGs (active variants of every rank/bucket), increasing bundle size and the Images registration cost at style load (search-map.tsx:665-676) — the halo-overlay alternative avoids this. (2) If the highlighted sprite id is ever missing from PIN_BADGE_IMAGES, Mapbox renders a blank icon (no implicit fallback inside the 'case' branch) — keep the fallback INSIDE the highlighted branch too, or guard the resolver. (3) Must confirm the highlighted pin still shows the correct (unchanged) rank number so selection recolors WITHOUT renumbering — this intersects the parallel goal that selection never changes badge rank. (4) Product-visible: only the selected pin changes; backward-compatible since label/dot already behave this way, so the pin will now match them (consistency improvement). (5) HMR caveat from CLAUDE.md: validate against a fresh FULL Metro bundle + cold launch, not an HMR patch, and confirm via the LOD harness rather than a screenshot."

plan.summary: Goal: rank BOTH the map pins and the results list by a high-precision Crave score (percentile_rank DESC, tie-break display_score DESC, then stable restaurantId) so the map badge number equals the list position; make tap-selection never reorder the badge rank (only native force-promote + recolor); and fix the selected-pin color bug where the pin sprite never recolors on highlight. The high-precision value (PublicEntityScore.percentileRank, Decimal(6,5), non-nullable, already in schema.prisma:182) is currently dropped in the score CTEs and never reaches mobile. Plan: (A) plumb percentile_rank through the API as a new additive optional field craveScoreExact on the shared FoodResult/RestaurantResult types and stamp it from the live dual-query path + coverage + favorites; (B) on mobile, thread craveScoreExact into feature.properties, re-key the map candidate sort to craveScoreExact (removing source-priority/rank from the ORDER), make the list a pure consumer of the API rank; (C) fix the pin highlight color by adding a nativeHighlighted case branch + active-color sprite. The single biggest open decision is whether the API also changes ORDER BY/rank to the high-precision key (cleanest, keeps list trivially in sync) or keeps current order and lets mobile re-rank — this changes user-visible order and needs the owner. Recommended field name: craveScoreExact across shared types + all set sites, sent as a raw Number(Decimal) (0..1, higher=better), never rounded. Only the live executeDual path is wired (single-query execute() and its mappers are dead code); the plan targets the live path and notes dead paths as optional.

orderedSteps.step: 1. Add the high-precision wire field to the shared serialization contract. Add `craveScoreExact?: number` to FoodResult and RestaurantResult; leave RestaurantFoodSnippet alone unless topFood ever needs independent ranking. Optional so deprecated/detail paths still typecheck. NestJS returns these plain objects so the field ships automatically once populated.
orderedSteps.change: Add `craveScoreExact?: number; // high-precision percentile_rank (0..1), higher=better; used for map/list rank agreement` after craveScore in FoodResult (line 47) and in RestaurantResult (after line 117).

orderedSteps.step: 2. Rebuild packages/shared so apps/api and apps/mobile both see the new field (apps consume the built dist, editing src without rebuild desyncs TS).
orderedSteps.change: Run the shared package build (e.g. `pnpm --filter @crave/shared build` or repo equivalent) and confirm dist types include craveScoreExact.

orderedSteps.step: 3. Plumb percentile_rank at the lowest common point in the SQL builder so all downstream CTEs can read it. Add `percentile_rank,` to the SELECT in BOTH the Prisma `sql` and the `preview` string of each CTE (preview is logging-only; a miss is cosmetic but keep them in sync).
orderedSteps.change: buildPublicRestaurantScoresCte (sql ~line 1401, preview ~1418) and buildPublicConnectionScoresCte (sql ~1435, preview ~1452): add `percentile_rank` to each SELECT alongside display_score/score_delta_7d.

orderedSteps.step: 4. Alias the high-precision column in the restaurant LIST CTE. Do NOT change ORDER BY (resolveRestaurantOrderSql stays display_score DESC, total_upvotes DESC, entity_id ASC).
orderedSteps.change: ranked_restaurants CTE: add `prs.percentile_rank AS crave_score_exact,` next to `prs.display_score AS crave_score` in both the sql (~line 261) and the preview (~line 304).

orderedSteps.step: 5. Alias the high-precision columns in the map PIN (dish) CTE. Do NOT change ORDER BY (resolveDishOrderSql unchanged).
orderedSteps.change: filtered_connections CTE: add `pcs.percentile_rank AS connection_crave_score_exact,` next to connection_crave_score (~line 569) and `prs.percentile_rank AS restaurant_crave_score_exact,` next to restaurant_crave_score (~line 581), in both the sql and the preview (~lines 611/617).

orderedSteps.step: 6. Extend the executor row interfaces to declare the new columns so TS sees them off the raw rows.
orderedSteps.change: RestaurantQueryRow (~121-161): add `crave_score_exact: ...` (Decimal/number, matching the existing crave_score type). DishQueryRow (~166-202): add `connection_crave_score_exact` (and optionally `restaurant_crave_score_exact`) with the same type as the existing score fields.

orderedSteps.step: 7. Populate craveScoreExact on the LIST DTO. Keep the server-assigned `rank = rankStart + index` (line 2392) unchanged — the API does NOT change order; mobile rank/sort is what aligns.
orderedSteps.change: mapRestaurantQueryResults (~2395): add `craveScoreExact: this.toRequiredPublicScore(row.crave_score_exact, \`restaurant:${row.restaurant_id}\`),` to the returned RestaurantResultDto (toRequiredPublicScore is Decimal-aware, no rounding).

orderedSteps.step: 8. Populate craveScoreExact on the MAP-PIN (food) DTO.
orderedSteps.change: mapDishQueryResults (~2471): add `craveScoreExact: this.toRequiredPublicScore(row.connection_crave_score_exact, \`connection:${row.connection_id}\`),` to the returned FoodResultDto.

orderedSteps.step: 9. Plumb percentile_rank through the COVERAGE (dots) GeoJSON path, which is a separate builder. Add to inline score CTEs, the main SELECT alias, the top-dish join, the row type, and the stamped properties. Coverage uses ad-hoc property objects (not the shared type) so no shared-type change is needed there, but mirror the craveScoreExact naming.
orderedSteps.change: Add `percentile_rank` to the inline public_restaurant_scores / public_connection_scores CTEs (~244-253); add `prs.percentile_rank AS crave_score_exact` to the main SELECT (~259); add `pcs.percentile_rank AS crave_score_exact` in buildTopDishJoinSql (~369); add `crave_score_exact` (+ `top_food_crave_score_exact` if includeTopDish) to CoverageRestaurantRow (~12-23); stamp `properties.craveScoreExact` (and `topDishCraveScoreExact`) in the GeoJSON properties (~314-333). Leave ORDER BY display_score unchanged.

orderedSteps.step: 10. Plumb percentileRank through the FAVORITES path (also renders on the map, builds the same RestaurantResult/FoodResult shapes).
orderedSteps.change: Add `'percentileRank'` to the FavoritePublicScore Pick (~52-55); add `percentileRank: true` to both Prisma selects in loadPublicScores (~625-636); set `craveScoreExact: Number(score.percentileRank)` in mapRestaurantResults (~725) and mapFoodResults (~790).

orderedSteps.step: 11. (OPTIONAL, owner decision — see Open Questions) If the API is to be the single source of order+rank: change resolveRestaurantOrderSql and resolveDishOrderSql to `percentile_rank DESC, display_score DESC, <stable id> ASC` and keep rank = rankStart+index. This makes restaurant.rank and the array order the high-precision order, so the list needs ZERO client change and the map+list agree trivially. SKIP this step if keeping current user-visible API order; in that case mobile re-ranks (steps 13-15).
orderedSteps.change: resolveRestaurantOrderSql (~1590-1595) and resolveDishOrderSql (~1567-1572): prepend `prs.percentile_rank DESC` / `fc.connection_crave_score_exact DESC` as the primary ORDER BY key, keep display_score and the existing stable id as tie-breaks. Coverage ORDER BY (~178-179) similarly if dots must match.

orderedSteps.step: 12. Add the high-precision field to the mobile feature properties type.
13. Stamp craveScoreExact onto features in the read-model builder (both the dish branch and the restaurant branch).
orderedSteps.change: Dish branch properties (~91-101): add `craveScoreExact: dish.craveScoreExact ?? null,`. Restaurant branch properties (~143-150): add `craveScoreExact: restaurant.craveScoreExact ?? null,`.

orderedSteps.step: 14. Re-key the map candidate SORT (not the dedup) so position == pure Crave-score rank. Remove VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY and feature.properties.rank from THIS comparator entirely; sort by craveScoreExact DESC, tie-break craveScore (display) DESC, then a stable id (restaurantId then markerKey). Missing exact must coalesce to sort last (deterministic).
orderedSteps.change: collectSearchMapVisualCandidates comparator (375-386): replace with `(coalesce(right.exact) - coalesce(left.exact)) || (right.display - left.display) || left.restaurantId.localeCompare(right.restaurantId) || left.markerKey.localeCompare(right.markerKey)`, reading exact from feature.properties.craveScoreExact and display from feature.properties.craveScore. No change to the re-index sites (1519, 1687) — they already do rank=index+1 over this now-Crave-ordered array, so catalog rank (→ native promotion line 1557 / Swift:1437), unifiedRankByKey (1694), promotedSeedKeys (1736), and badge rank (1782-1794) all become the pure Crave-score rank automatically and stay mutually consistent.

orderedSteps.step: 15. KEEP VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY only in the dedup winner choice. Leave shouldReplaceVisualCandidate (316-330), VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY (288-294), and resolveEffectiveVisualSourceKind (296-314) UNCHANGED — they decide which duplicate feature/coordinate (selected vs viewport vs coverage) wins the single visual-identity slot, which is identity not order. Once the sort ignores priority, flipping a feature to 'selected'/'restaurant_only' no longer moves its rank, so tap-selection stops renumbering the badge (the selection-decouple goal) with no further JS change.
orderedSteps.change: No edit; verify these three blocks are untouched and that the re-projection path (2669-2727) passes craveScoreExact through baked properties for coverage-sourced features (add passthrough at ~2701-2725 if missing).

orderedSteps.step: 16. Make the RESULTS LIST agree with the map. If step 11 (API rank by percentile_rank) is taken, NO list change is needed — the list already renders in API array order and reads row.rank (search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:512-514 and :1097). If step 11 is SKIPPED, re-rank the list client-side using the SAME comparator as the map so both surfaces use one ordering.
orderedSteps.change: If keeping current API order: in buildSafeResultsData (43-60) add a single stable sort by the same craveScoreExact DESC / display DESC / restaurantId ASC comparator (restaurants only; dishes unaffected), and reassign the badge index accordingly so list position == badge == map rank. If step 11 is taken, leave this file untouched (pure API-order consumer).

orderedSteps.step: 17. Fix the selected-pin COLOR bug: add a nativeHighlighted case branch to the pin icon expression (mirrors the dot at search-map.tsx:2390 and the label at use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts:2933). The native feature-state IS already written to pinBundleSourceId (Swift applyHighlightedMarkerState 9519-9573) — no native change needed; the pin layer just never reads it.
orderedSteps.change: stylePinSingleSymbolStyle.iconImage (2623-2645): wrap as `['case', nativeHighlightedExpression, <highlighted-pin-image>, ['coalesce', ['get','badgeImageId'], <existing score-bucket step fallback>]]`, reusing the existing nativeHighlightedExpression (2378-2381). Keep a fallback INSIDE the highlighted branch (missing sprite renders blank). Add nativeHighlightedExpression to the useMemo deps (~2660).

orderedSteps.step: 18. Provide the highlighted pin SPRITE that preserves the rank number (selection must NOT renumber). Bake an active-color variant per rank/bucket and a resolver; register and list the ids. (Cheaper alternative noted in risks: a single active-color halo/ring overlay in a second pin layer gated on nativeHighlightedExpression, leaving the numbered badge untouched — pick if a per-rank bake is too heavy.)
orderedSteps.change: generate-pin-bucket-sprites.js: add an active-palette pass (canonical colors ~58-65 + an ACTIVE color) emitting e.g. `pin-rank-active-<rank>`. quality-color.ts: add `activeRankBadgeImageId(rank)` sibling to rankBadgeImageId (118-125). pin-badge-images.ts: include the new ids. search-map.tsx MapboxGL.Images (665-676): register the new sprites. Do NOT use iconColor — pin badge PNGs are non-SDF full-color (registered without sdf), so iconColor is a no-op.

orderedSteps.step: 19. Validate end-to-end on a FRESH FULL Metro bundle + cold launch (not an HMR patch), confirm the installed binary is newer than source edits, and use the LOD harness as source of truth.
orderedSteps.change: Force a full bundle, cold-launch, and via the [lodev] harness + [RANKDIAG-v2] probe (use-direct-search-map-source-controller.ts ~1526-1533) confirm: (a) map badge #1 == list #1 (the real top, e.g. Mia's, not the formerly-tied Caffè Panna), (b) tapping a pin no longer makes its mapRank #1, (c) the tapped pin's painted sprite switches to the active color while roleP/renderP and badge rank are unchanged, (d) no zoom wiggle (bundle:[*,*,0] during movement) since badge sprite ids changed.

apiChanges: packages/shared/src/types/search.ts — add optional `craveScoreExact?: number` to FoodResult (~47) and RestaurantResult (~117); rebuild packages/shared dist (apps consume built types).

NO native (Swift) change required for the color fix — applyHighlightedMarkerState already writes nativeHighlighted to pinBundleSourceId (9519-9573), invoked on selection (2959) and per-frame (2493); the pin layer just needed the expression branch.
openQuestions: PRIMARY DECISION — should the API change ORDER BY and the server-assigned `rank` to the high-precision key (percentile_rank DESC, display_score DESC, stable id)? Option A (yes): cleanest, list needs zero client change, map+list agree trivially, but it changes the user-visible search result ORDER for ALL clients (web, favorites, coverage share the same scores). Option B (no, keep current order): API stays additive-only, mobile re-ranks both surfaces client-side; old clients keep the (buggy) display_score order. The whole goal is achievable either way but they differ in blast radius — owner must choose.

Confirm the intended UX changes are acceptable: badge numbers WILL shift for any restaurant whose percentile_rank order differs from its display_score order (e.g. the current top can change), and the tapped pin will no longer jump to #1. Both are intended fixes but product-visible.

Field naming: ship the wire field as `craveScoreExact` (recommended, consistent with display craveScore) vs `percentileRank` (matches the DB column). Pick one and use it verbatim across shared types + all set sites. Value is the raw Decimal(6,5) fraction 0..1 (higher=better) sent as a Number, not rounded — confirm mobile compares it numerically only for ordering (not displayed).

Highlighted-pin sprite strategy: full per-rank active-color bake (preserves the number, adds ~1683 PNGs + larger Images registration cost) vs a single active-color halo/ring overlay in a second pin layer (cheap, leaves the numbered badge untouched, 'recolor' = an active ring rather than a full body repaint). Which reading of 'recolor' does the owner want?
Should RestaurantFoodSnippet (topFood) also carry craveScoreExact? Not required for the map/list goal; only needed if topFood ever needs independent high-precision ranking.
plan.risks: USER-VISIBLE ORDER: The only change that alters search order is the optional API ORDER BY/rank step (step 11). percentile_rank (Decimal(6,5)) distinguishes far more finely than display_score (Decimal(5,1)), so near-ties (~99.9) WILL re-order vs today — this is the intended fix but shifts badge numbers and list positions for tied restaurants; if step 11 is skipped, the API order is unchanged and only mobile re-ranks. Either way, the comparator MUST end on a deterministic stable id (restaurantId then markerKey) on BOTH map and list, or pins/list disagree frame-to-frame. BACKWARD-COMPAT OF THE API FIELD: craveScoreExact is additive and optional — older mobile clients ignore it and keep the existing craveScore/rank/order, so adding it is safe and non-breaking; until mobile re-ranks, behavior is identical (bug persists by design on old clients). percentile_rank is NON-nullable for scored rows so toRequiredPublicScore won't throw for live data (same failure surface as today's craveScore). OTHER RISKS: (1) add the new column in BOTH the Prisma sql AND the preview string of every CTE or the debug preview diverges (cosmetic only — preview isn't executed). (2) KEEP VISUAL_SOURCE_PRIORITY in shouldReplaceVisualCandidate — dropping it there would change which duplicate feature/coordinate wins for the selected restaurant and could move the selected pin's location (multi-location pick). (3) Rows with missing/non-finite rank are silently dropped at search-mounted-results-data-store.ts:1098-1100, so the API must always assign rank (the existing rankStart+index keeps doing this). (4) Removing the selection re-rank is safe because native promotion of the tapped pin rides highlightedMarkerKeys (search-map.tsx:2049), independent of the JS sort — the demoted-but-selected pin still force-promotes. (5) Color fix is low blast radius (additive case branch; non-highlighted branch byte-identical) but if the active sprite id is missing Mapbox renders blank — keep a fallback inside the highlighted branch; the per-rank bake adds bundle size (halo alternative avoids it). (6) Rebuild packages/shared dist or TS desyncs across apps. (7) Verify the precomputed catalog path (map-read-model-builder.ts orderByEntry ~24-36 + compute-marker-pipeline.ts canonicalRestaurantRankById ~49-58) orders by the SAME key as the fallback, else precomputed vs fallback catalogs rank differently. (8) Validate against a FRESH FULL Metro bundle + cold launch (not HMR) and via the LOD harness, since badge sprite ids change — confirm no zoom wiggle and badge==promotion rank.
